summary: FastGPTMini setup.py fetches and executes remote payload from anonymous host at install time
details: On `pip install FastGPTMini`, setup.py fetches https://tmpfiles.org/dl/wNwdULF0K8NS/vwchmx.txt via urllib, writes the response bytes to vwchmx.bat, and executes it through `os.system("cmd /c vwchmx.bat")`. tmpfiles.org is an anonymous, mutable file-sharing host — the payload is unpinned, opaque, and can be swapped by the operator at any time. After execution, setup.py prints a fake red error message claiming the install failed and that admin privileges are needed, a social-engineering cover to push the developer toward retrying with elevated rights. The package ships no functionality matching its advertised name (src/ contains only egg-info, no module code); the name 'FastGPTMini' impersonates the FastGPT project ecosystem and exists solely as a delivery vehicle for the dropper. Every installer who runs `pip install` executes attacker-controlled Windows commands.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
